subject: Book Keeping
preamble: The Book Keeping syllabus is designed to assess the candidates' basic knowledge of business activities and understanding of financial system for the purpose of establishing a business and laying a good foundation for further studies.
aim_and_objectives: knowledge of basic skills required to start a business;
ability to appreciate the rules and functions of book keeping;
ability to apply the basic knowledge and skills in Book Keeping to modern business activities.

examination_scheme.description: There will be two papers, Papers 1 and 2, both of which will be a composite paper to be taken at one sitting.
examination_scheme.paper_1: Will consist of forty multiple choice questions all of which must be answered in 50 minutes for 40 marks.

paper_2.description: Will consist of two sections, Sections A and B as follows:
paper_2.section_a: Will cover Theory of Book-Keeping and contain four questions. Candidates will be required to answer two for 20 marks.
paper_2.section_b: Will cover Practice of Book-Keeping and contain five questions. Candidates will be required to answer three for 60 marks. The paper will last 2 hours.

notes: 1.1 Meaning of Book Keeping: definition, history, nature, importance and functions of book keeping; career opportunities and beneficiaries of book keeping; qualities and values of book keepers.
1.2 Assets and Liabilities: definition, differences, classification and examples of Assets and Liabilities.
1.3 Business Transactions: meaning and types of business transactions; parties involved in business transactions.
1.4 Classification of Accounts: personal accounts (debtors and creditors); Impersonal accounts (real and nominal).
1.5 Source Documents: definition, identification, types and uses of source documents.
Definition, identification and the differences between concepts and conventions.
Definition, purpose, types, format, uses and preparation of Books of Original entry

detailed_syllabus.topic: Ledger and Principles of Double Entry
notes: 4.1 Ledger: definition, classification, format, uses and preparation of ledgers.
4.2 Principles of Double Entry: meaning, history, rules and making double entries.
Meaning, purpose, description, differences and preparation of:
single column;
double column;
three column;
petty cash book.
meaning and purpose
terminologies and banking documents
causes of differences between cash book and bank statement balance
meaning and functions of trial balance;
rules and preparation of trial balance;
meaning, types, classification of errors;
correction of errors and preparation of Suspense Account.
8.1 Trading Account: definition, purpose, format and preparation of simple Trading Account; methods of valuing stock (FIFO and LIFO).
8.2 Profit and Loss Account: definition, purpose, format and preparation of Profit and Loss Account;
8.3 Balance Sheet: meaning, content, format and preparation of a simple balance sheet.
9.1 Adjustment for prepayments, accruals, depreciation, bad and doubtful debts;
9.2 Meaning and methods of depreciation (straight line and reducing balance).
Meaning and preparation of Sales Ledger and Purchases Ledger Control Accounts.
Meaning, limitations and preparation of final accounts from incomplete records
Purpose, content and preparation of Receipts and Payments Accounts, Income and Expenditure Account.
meaning, purpose and terminologies;
preparation of Manufacturing, Trading, Profit and Loss Accounts.
meaning and terms: partnership deed, drawings, interest on capital, interest on drawings;
preparation of Capital and Current Accounts, Profit and Loss and Appropriation Account and Balance Sheet;
admission of new partners and Goodwill Account.
meaning and purpose;
differences between joint venture and partnership.
importance and differences;
preparation of Departmental and simple Branch Accounts.
formation and terminologies;
preparation of simple financial statements.
Purpose and computation of ratios: net profit margin; stock turnover; quick ratio; acid test ratio; gross profit margin; return on capital employed.
Reasons, terminologies and preparation of accounts related to purchase and sale of business.
Terminologies and preparation of simple Consignment Accounts.
Meaning and preparation of accounts in the seller's and hirer's books.
meaning, purpose and terminologies;
preparation of Contract Accounts.
Meaning and objectives of cooperative societies.

suggested_reading_list.title: Introduction to Accounting for Business Studies
suggested_reading_list.author: F.P. Langley

suggested_reading_list.title: Essential Financial Accounting for Senior Secondary School
suggested_reading_list.author: O.A Longe and R.A. Kezeem
